kata.title: Binary Tree Maximum Path Sum
kata.workload: post-order clamp-DP (gain(c)=max(0,up(c)); through=val+gain(L)+gain(R); answer=max through over all nodes; running max threaded in a mut ref i64) over a forest of T=2048 balanced mixed-sign trees of N=511 RC nodes, built ONCE, then K=60 SERIAL passes folding a sum-of-results sink

env.note: Measured on a shared x86-64 Linux cloud container (karac from kara main), NOT the corpus's Apple M5 Pro. Absolute times/sizes/RSS NOT comparable across hosts; only within-file cross-language ratios are the signal. Single-threaded RC-tree post-order traversal, --warmup 8 --runs 50, seq lane (fastest first): c < kara < go < rust ~ rust(ovf). Instruction-count decomposition (cachegrind, mini workload): c 73.8M vs kara 118.1M vs rust(Box) 116.2M — clang emits ~1.6x leaner recursion codegen, which is c's edge. kara and rustc-O emit near-identical instruction counts yet kara runs ~2x faster in wall-clock; cachegrind's modelled caches do not account for it, so the kara-over-rust margin is a real-hardware memory-behaviour effect not fully root-caused here — reported as a measured observation, not a language-superiority claim. Equal-safety: kara's default overflow+unwrap checks line up with the rustc -O -C overflow-checks=on row (the two rust rows are within noise, so the safety tax is negligible on this pointer-bound workload).
